content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing Ludo. According to the rules of Ludo, a player can enter a new token into the play only when he rolls a 666 on the die.

In the current turn, Chef rolled the number XXX on the die. Determine if Chef can enter a new token into the play in the current turn or not.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains one integer XXX — the number rolled by the Chef on the die.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if the Chef can enter a new token in the game. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the number rolled on the die.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output "YES" if X equals 6, otherwise output "NO". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: X=1 ≠ 6 → NO.\nTest case 2: X=6 → YES.\nTest case 3: X=3 ≠ 6 → NO.
Both rolls are 6, so Chef can enter a new token in each turn.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
YES
YES
YES
YES
YES
YES

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. Loop T times, reading X each iteration. If X is exactly 6, print YES; otherwise print NO. This is a direct O(1) check per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
